content.problemStatement:
For an array AAA, we define score(A)\text{score}(A)score(A) as ∑i=1∣A∣(−1)i⋅Ai\sum_{i = 1}^{|A|} (-1)^i \cdot A_i∑i=1∣A∣​(−1)i⋅Ai​, i.e. alternating sum of its elements. Note that the score of an empty array is 000.

Further, we define f(A)f(A)f(A) as the maximum value of score(B)\text{score}(B)score(B) over all BBB which are subsequences†^{\dagger}† of AAA. Here, it is allowed for BBB to be empty.

You are given an array of NNN integers - [A1,A2,…,AN][A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N][A1​,A2​,…,AN​] and QQQ queries:


Given LLL and RRR, find f([AL,AL+1,…,AR])f([A_L, A_{L + 1}, \ldots, A_R])f([AL​,AL+1​,…,AR​]).
†^{\dagger}† BBB is said to be a subsequence of AAA, if it can be formed by deleting several (possibly 000) elements from AAA without changing order of the remaining elements. For example, [][][], [1,3][1, 3][1,3] and [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3] are subsequences of [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3] but [3,1][3, 1][3,1] is not.


Input Format

The first line contains 222 integers NNN and QQQ - the size of the array and the number of queries.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The next QQQ lines each contain 222 integers - LLL and RRR.


Output Format
For each query, output f([AL,AL+1,…,AR])f([A_L, A_{L + 1}, \ldots, A_R])f([AL​,AL+1​,…,AR​]).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Query 1:  The optimal subsequence is B=[−10,5,−3,5]B = [-10, 5, -3, 5]B=[−10,5,−3,5] which has score(B)=10+5+3+5=23\text{score}(B) = 10 + 5 + 3 + 5 = 23score(B)=10+5+3+5=23.
Query 2: The optimal subsequence is B=[−10,5,−3]B = [-10, 5, -3]B=[−10,5,−3] which has score(B)=10+5+3=18\text{score}(B) = 10 + 5 + 3 = 18score(B)=10+5+3=18.
Query 3: The optimal subsequence BBB is empty here, which has score of 000.
